text: When designing a REST API, following best practices ensures it is easy to use and maintain. First, use clear and consistent endpoints that represent resources, such as `/users` or `/orders`. Status codes are crucial for communication—for example, `200 OK` for success, `404 Not Found` for missing resources, and `500 Internal Server Error` for server issues. Good documentation helps developers understand how to use your API effectively. Additionally, always include versioning, like `/v1/users`, to avoid breaking changes when you update the API. These practices make your API reliable, scalable, and developer-friendly.
